subject: Physics
title: Superconductors: Zero Resistance
description: A quick win: understand Superconductors: Zero Resistance.

hook.text: Cool certain materials below a critical temperature and electrical resistance vanishes completely. Current flows forever.

buildup.text: In 1911, Heike Kamerlingh Onnes cooled mercury to 4.2 K and found it had zero electrical resistance.

discovery.text: A current started in a superconducting loop will flow indefinitely with no energy loss. Perfect conduction.

twist.text: Superconductors also repel magnets—they levitate. This is the Meissner effect.

climax.text: MRI machines, particle accelerators, and maglev trains all depend on superconducting magnets.

punchline.text: Zero resistance. Floating magnets. Superconductors defy ordinary physics.

quiz.question: What happens to electrical resistance in a superconductor?
options: It drops to exactly zero below the critical temperature
